Only purchase orders or payrolls requested/signed by the Officer in Charge of the Solid Waste Management Program with the concurrence of the Local Chief Executive should be funded.
Also, we have recommended to the Management that the projects to be funded should conform with the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan for the calendar years 2016-2025 and to the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM, which is funding only environmental and developmental projects that shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Accountant to determine the unexpended/unutilized balance of QRF, DRRM-MOOE, and other funds received by the Municipality intended for supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the 5-year period and transfer the same to the special trust fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the trust fund books as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 before the year-end financial statements are finalized and submitted for audit.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Accountant to hasten preparation of report pertaining to the utilization of DRRMF in order that Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer can submit the report on or before 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU as this a requirement under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We have recommended to the Management that before the Budget Officer will make an obligation from the Fund, the purchase order or contract should come from the LDRRM Officer who is in-charge in the implementation of PPAs under the LDRRMF.
We have also recommended that the releases or uses of the 30% QRF should be covered by the SB resolution declaring the LGU under the state of calamity or a national declaration of state of calamity to avoid disallowance in audit.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should properly monitor all the disbursements charged to the Special Education Fund and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We have recommended that the Management  should comply strictly with Section 1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 and Section 4 of the Administrative Order (AO) No. 17 by procuring common-use supplies and equipment from PS-DBM, in support to the government’s policy for transparency in government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes.

We have recommended that Management should revert back the amount of P4.545 million to the revenue collected from excise tax on tobacco products and the above project should be funded by the General Fund under the 20% MDF.
We also recommended to the Management to explain why management should not be held liable under Article 220 of the Revised Penal Code for using the fund under excise tax on tobacco products for projects not beneficial to tobacco farmers. In the future, avoid misusing revenue collected from excise tax on tobacco products. Fund only the supposed projects falling under the criteria set forth in Section 2 of the Republic Act No. 7171.

We have recommended that Management should explain why Educational Assistance were granted to applicant college students for scholarship program even without a guideline which will serve as a parameter whether or not the applicants for the program are qualified and deserving.
We also recommended that Management should explain why the disbursement for educational assistance has exceeded the budgeted amount by P604,700.00.

We have recommended that department heads should  explain why the excess RATA they received in view of their absences incurred, may not be disallowed in audit.
We have recommended further that the Municipal Treasury Office should secure a certification on department head from Human Resource Department as basis for the payment of RATA.
Finally, we also recommended that the Municipality should  strictly adhere to the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of the department heads.

We have recommended that SB members should explain why the excess RATA they received in view of their absences incurred, may not be disallowed in audit.
We have recommended further that Municipal Treasury Office should  secure a certification on the attendance of the Honorable Body from the SB Secretary as basis for the payment of RATA.
We also recommended that the Municipality should strictly adhere to the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian.
Finally, we have recommended that the legislative body should prepare a work schedule or performance standards in compliance to DBM LBC No. 102 dated January 17, 2013, as amended by DBM LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 to facilitate the audit of payments of their RATA.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the HRMO should properly monitor and initiate the necessary controls on the salary deductions of the Municipality’s officials and employees by ensuring that it will not reduce their monthly net take home pay lower than the required amount provided in the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act.

We have  recommended that the Management should adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, Section 7.2.1 (a), prescribing the responsibility of the agency accountant on timely submission to COA of monthly financial reports and supporting documents.

We have recommended that the Accountant should make sure that supporting documents of DVs are complete before they are being paid by the Cashier or Municipal Treasurer.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should make sure that the Report of Checks Issued submitted to Accounting were supported with DVs with its pertinent documents before the Accounting will post them in Check Disbursement Journal. Submit to COA the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents together with check disbursement vouchers within the prescribed period.

We have recommended that the Chairman and the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should ensure that contracts and all its supporting documents, including those required in the auditorial and technical review, be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from its execution as required by COA Circular No. 2009-001. We also recommended that the above listed documents be submitted to COA for auditorial contract review.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers.
In addition, form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.
Finally, we also recommended that Management should comply strictly on the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.

We  have  recommended  that Management should ensure that the expenditures being charged to GAD fund should have a well-defined gender issue to be addressed.  Adopt an internal control for the preparation of a work and financial plan for each GAD project and activity to avoid being misuse.  Furnish a copy of the work and financial plan to Budget Officer for matching of GAD PPA against expenditures to be obligated.

We have recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections daily and intact to its authorized government depository bank in strict compliance with the above-cited provisions of MNGAS to safeguard collections from possible risk of loss or misuse
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer have tried her best effort to deposit all collections daily and intact.
observations.reason: Not all collections were deposited daily and intact as verified in the Report of Collections and Deposits.  Cash on hand was maintained to cater to emergency needs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to make all payments by check in order to strengthen the internal control system of the Municipality in handling cash.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash fund was set-up for cash payments.
observations.reason: Interview with Assistant Treasurer revealed that they have set-up Petty Cash Fund of P200,000.00.  However, due to financial assistance to constituents, scholars and petty expenses, petty cash fund runs low most often and as a result collections for were sometimes use for cash payments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to grant cash advances strictly in accordance with COA Circular 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445 and to cause the withholding of salaries and other amounts due the concerned officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until they have been fully settled. 

We also recommended that cash advances shall be granted only after settlement or proper accounting of previous ones are made.
observations.managementAction: The Management made efforts to informed those officers and employees to liquidated their cash advances.
observations.reason: The cash advances granted to officers and employees totaling P221,450 were liquidated in the first  quarter of 2016 leaving a balance of P16,480 as of December 31, 2016 as unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the assignment of concerned personnel (i) to conduct the inventory of supplies and materials inventory semi-annually, (ii) after completion of the physical count, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RRCI) thereon; and (iii) reconcile said report with the records of the Municipal Accounting Office, in conformance with Section 124, Volume I of NGAS Manual.  
Direct the Municipal Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories; 
Instruct the Property & Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections, if necessary; and require Property & Supply Officer to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant for recording.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory of Other Supplies was conducted in the SF-PTA but not in the Municipality.  Central Procurement of office supplies is not in placed. Each department procure its own office supplies.
observations.reason:
The inventory account amounting to P27,690 refers only to inventory of supplies in  SF-PTA Beach Resort and not in the Municipality.

As of December 31, 2016, perpetual inventory system is not yet in placed as well as the maintenance of stock cards and supply ledger card.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to reconstitute the Inventory Committee which should also include a representative from the Accounting Office in conducting a complete physical inventory of all PPE with the results thereof contained in a duly prepared and approved RPCPPE in order to ascertain the accuracy of the reported balance of the PPE account in the financial statement.

We recommended that the Property & Supply Officer and all other concerned municipal officials take appropriate action to secure proof of ownership of all lands owned by the Municipality. Also, require the Municipal Accountant, together with the Inventory Committee, thru the General Services Office to reconcile their respective records to come up with complete, accurate and reliable Land account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property & Supply Officer be required to maintain Real Property Ledger Cards and Real Property Cards for land respectively, to support the Land account

d)  We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed and as a supporting document of the reported PPE account in the financial Not statement
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory of PPEs is on-going.
observations.reason:
Members of the Inventory Committee were only selected verbally and not by way of an office order.  

The conduct of the inventory was done beyond the cut-off date of December 31, 2016 and no report of inventory was submitted before January 31, 2017 to COA.
  
For the securing of land title, fund was being sourced for this activity.

The Accounting department has an e-copy of Property Ledger Card and was being updated from time to time.

observations.recommendation:
a) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit paid vouchers and supporting documents totalling P33,331,507.11, in addition to all unsubmitted DVs from October 2015 to December 2015, within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to certify claims only with complete  supporting documents  and for the Cashier or Municipal Treasurer to ensure that paid vouchers submitted to the Office of the Auditor are properly supported with complete supporting documents.

b) We also recommended that from hereon, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all claims be supported with complete supporting documents and properly approved before payment.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers were being submitted.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the unsubmitted vouchers totalled P6.4 million.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Development Council to comply strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain the desired socio-economic development and improvement of environmental condition for its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Charges made with the 20% Development Fund refers to salaries, wages and gasoline expenses that pertains to solid waste management program.
observations.reason: The Management is not yet strict in the implementation of this recommendation.  Still there were charges made which does not support the priority projects and activities provided under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2011-1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to determine the unexpended/unutilized balance of QRF, DRRM-MOOE, and other funds received by the Municipality intended for supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the 5-year period and transfer the same to the special trust fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the trust fund books as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, before the year-end financial statements are finalized and submitted for audit.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for the purpose of monitoring the utilization of the Fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. 

We have recommended that Management first secure a Local Sanggunian Resolution declaring the Municipality in a state of calamity or a Presidential declaration of state of calamity upon recommendation of the NDRRMC before the release and use of the 30% Quick Response Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Special trust fund was set-up for unexpended balance of LDRRMF in December 2016.

The release and use  QRF was always supported by SB Res.
observations.reason: Verification showed that Special trust fund was set up for unexpended balance of LDRRMF in December 2016.  However, the submission of monthly report of the utilization of LDRRMFund was not done yet.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive 

that through the appropriate management mechanism of Municipal Health Office to optimally utilize the Per Family Payment Rate Fund in accordance with the utilization set forth under Philhealth Circular No. 10 series of 2012 to ensure all agency’s constituents a better access to quality health services that are efficiently delivered, equitably distributed, fairly financed and appropriately utilized by an informed and empowered public.

instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account used for Philhealth capitation from Due to NGAs to Due to Other GOCCs in pursuant to COA Circular No. 2004-008.
observations.managementAction:
The Management have tried their best to hasten the utilization of the PFPR fund.
The Accounting have reclassify Philhealth account from Due to NGAs to Due to Other GOCCs.
observations.reason: Verification showed that RHU have drawn out programs and projects ahead of receiving the fund as a means of hastening the utilization of the fund. Also the Accounting have  reclassify Philhealth account from Due to NGAs to Due to Other GOCCs

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local School Board limit the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects defined under RA 7160 and to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant that the scholarship grants be charged against the General Fund of the Municipality instead of Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management have noted the recommendation that TESDA related scholarship should be charged under the General Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was fully aware now of where to charged scholarship grants of TESDA Scholars.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account Food Supplies to the appropriate expense account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant have well noted the recommendation and she assured the team to not use the Food Supplies account for meals and snacks.
observations.reason: Verification of the entries made in the Food Supplies account for the year 2016 refers to supplementary feeding program for day care centers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should require the submission of the supporting documents from the BAC Secretariat as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the payment of honoraria of BAC members.
observations.managementAction: The claims for honoraria was supported by Minutes of Opening of Bids.
observations.reason: Still the supporting documents for the claims for honorarium are insufficient.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
 a.  require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 43 on the Manual on NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly trial balances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006
observations.managementAction: Efforts have been exerted to submit reports within the deadline set by COA but the Treasury department lags behind.
observations.reason: The Accounting was delayed in the submission of their monthly financial reports by two to three months.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the contracts and its supporting documents be submitted to the Auditor’s Office within five calendar days as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001. 

We have also recommended that the GSO submit copies of Pos, Acceptance and Inspection Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Great effort were exerted to submit to COA the POs and Contracts within the 5 days grace period.
observations.reason: There were still POs and Contracts which were submitted beyond the 5 days grace period.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9184 in order to promote transparency in the procurement process aimed at reducing procurement costs. 

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive should designate a dedicated office-in-charge of procuring activities to ensure that effective planning and implementation of procurement activities are achieved
observations.managementAction:
Not all department of the Municipality purchase their own office supplies from PS-DBM due to unavailability of some office supplies.  Thus, most opted to procure from other sources.  

The consolidation of office supplies to be procured is under consideration.
observations.reason: Procurement of office supplies and other supplies was not yet centralized.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Government continue its drive against solid waste disposal by promoting the waste minimization programs and increasing the educational campaign through proper waste management to attain cleanliness and ensure the health of every individual

We also recommended that the Municipal Government effectively monitor the implementation of the Programs, Projects and Activities of the 10-year solid waste management plans and observe with the guidelines provided in Republic Act No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program
observations.managementAction: The Management is pursuing construction of the material recovery facility for recycled waste products.
observations.reason: Material Recovery Facility is in the early stage of implementation  yet.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to advise the Treasurer’s Office to exert more effort in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers and to form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes. We also recommend to impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes
observations.managementAction: Collection was in full force however some RPT taxpayers were not willing to pay their dues.
observations.reason: Validation showed that there were delinquent taxpayers as of December 2016 based on the reports submitted by the Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure the full implementation of the programs, projects and activities of GAD to fully achieve the GAD mandate in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Office was vigilant of the charges made to GAD funds.
observations.reason: There were still charges which were not GAD-related.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting and Treasury coordinate closely and make proper adjustments on the subject receivables in order that the accounting records jibe with the treasury records to reflect the correct amount of receivables. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was done between the Accounting and Treasury department and adjustments were recorded in books of account.

observations.recommendation:
Collections were not deposited intact, thus, Cash In Vault account at year end showed an excessive balance of ₱796,910.32, thereby exposing government funds to possible misuse/loss

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit her daily collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day to ensure proper safeguard of cash and to avoid its possible misuse/application or loss.

Collections in the amount of ₱5,431,777.08 was used for cash payments thereby, internal control system in handling cash was weak.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to make all payments by check and increase the amount of cash advance for petty cash fund in order to strengthen the internal control system of the agency in handling cash.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash fund was set up to cater to cash payments.
observations.reason: Interview with Assistant Treasurer revealed that they have set-up Petty Cash Fund of P200,000.00.  However, due to financial assistance to constituents, scholars and petty expenses, petty cash fund runs most often and they sometimes used the collections for cash payments.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Agency Officials procure all office supplies from the PSDBM to comply with Section 53(e) of Republic Act 9184 and to obtain reasonable prices advantageous to the municipality
observations.managementAction: Every department is responsible in the procurement of their office supplies and they also purchase from DBM Procurement Services.
observations.reason: Results of validation showed that only 5% of the office supplies were purchase from PS-DBM and 95% were procured from other sources.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Agency adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 96-010, respectively, for submission within five (5) days after issuance of all purchase order, contracts to the office of the Auditor and to include all the needed documents needed for review and evaluation. In like manner, Management must also notified to the Auditor within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of all deliveries.
observations.managementAction: The Management have exerted efforts  to submit the Purchase Orders and Contracts within the time frame set by COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 96-010, however, some contracts were not submitted on time.
observations.reason: Some contracts and purchase orders were received by COA beyond the 5days grace period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of the law.  In addition,update the submission of the collection and disbursement vouchers together with cash receipts journal, cash disbursement journals, check disbursement journals and other related reports.
observations.managementAction: The Management was late in the submission of financial reports.
observations.reason: Still the management was late in the submission of financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the head of the agency require  the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to comply with the preparation and submission of the monthly sources and utilization of the LDRRMF  in compliance with the Section 5.1.5 of  COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO on the monthly submission of the sources and utilization of LDRRMF to COA.
observations.reason: The Management fail to submit monthly report on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF to COA.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the unexpended balances of the two disaster related funds to the  Trust Fund books in accordance with the above cited recommended accounting entries as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Trust account was set up for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: The setting up of trust account for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF was only done in December 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management shall keep a bank account for the Supplementary Feeding Program not only as a compliance to the DSWD Administrative Order 08, series 2012 but also to sustain the maintenance of health feeding program of the government and help realize the nutritional needs of the school children for a better school attendance and assist them in their learning process.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to open account solely for DSWD account for the supplemental Feeding Program for School Children.
observations.reason: An LBP account was established for the Supplemental Feeding Program and other DSWD programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen the internal control system of the municipality regarding the collection and recording of the Real Property Tax.  Enforce collection from the delinquent taxpayers by sending RPTOP’s and notice of delinquency to taxpayers.  Intensify collection in order to improve tax performance efficiency. Conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes. Coordinate with the barangay officials so as to collect all taxes on real property located within their jurisdiction. Records management be done separately by different persons. Comply with the provision embodied in the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes
observations.managementAction: The Management have tried their best in collecting taxes however some were not cooperative in paying their tax obligation.
observations.reason: The Management lagged in the collection of real property taxes due to lack of manpower resources for collection and updating of records for RPT and unwillingness of some real property taxpayers to settle their dues.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that appropriate charges be filed against the erring accountable officers for the malversation of public funds pursuant to Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code, as amended.

A strong internal control system be installed and existing laws, rules and regulations be strictly observed in the handling of cash in order to avoid the occurrence of cash shortage and to safeguard the government funds from losses, misuse, misapplication/ misappropriation.

We have further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discharge her duties efficiently and effectively and supervise/monitor the revenue collectors and the liquidating officer in the performance of their duties.
observations.managementAction: The management have suspended these erring employees for 60 days and have exerted effort to collect the involved amount due from them.
observations.reason: One of the erring officials was made to restitute the amount malversed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that henceforth, she refrain from using her collections to pay the expenses of the municipality and strictly comply with the general principle of Imprest System as required under Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 by making use of checks for all payments, except for small amounts which may be paid through petty cash fund, and by depositing her collections intact and daily with the authorized depository bank to prevent misapplication/misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Petty Cash Fund was set up for cash payment.
observations.reason: Interview with Assistant Treasurer revealed that they have set-up Petty Cash Fund of P200,000.00.  However, due to financial assistance to constituents, scholars and petty expenses, petty cash fund runs most often and they sometimes used the collections for cash payments.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management refrain from charging expenditures which are not related or connected with the implementation of development projects. Furthermore, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer assist the Local Chief Executive in determining the developmental projects, programs and activities which is consistent with the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The salaries and wages, gasoline  and repair and maintenance of back hoe and dump truck which were charged under the 20% MDF refers to the monitoring and maintenance of the open dumpsite of the Municipality under – Environment Purposes – Waste Management System.
observations.reason: The salaries and wages, gasoline  and repair and maintenance of back hoe and dump truck which were continually charged under the 20% MDF. Such were item of expenditures under the Solid Waste Management Program.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials exert more effort to increase PTA income and closely monitor, as well as, limit the expenses equivalent to how much was collected to avoid a deficit. We have further recommended that a feasibility study be made by the management regarding their plan to lease the property to private entity or persons to avoid possible problems that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: In the recent months, the San Fabian PTA Beach Resort  is improving its operations brought about by re-organization in the SF-PTA management, increasing income due to increase of customers, thus improvements were being done on the site.
observations.reason: Interview made with the Accountant revealed that San Fabian-PTA Beach Resort was earning due to improvements of facilities and amenities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the local government unit adopt the remedies/measures provided in Section 254 of Republic Act 7160 to intensify its collection of real property taxes from delinquent taxpayers and exert more effort in the collection of stall rentals in order to realize additional income that could be used for the implementation of vital projects, programs and activities geared toward the welfare of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management have collected the due delinquent stall rentals however there were still delinquent real property taxes.
observations.reason: There were still delinquent real property taxes as of December 31, 2016.  Delinquent stall rentals were collected in the 2016.
